checks.fix: Rotate the key immediately at console.anthropic.com. Move to environment variable or secret manager.
checks.reason: Live Anthropic API key committed to the repo. Immediately rotatable but must be rotated now.

checks.fix: Rotate at platform.openai.com/api-keys. Use OPENAI_API_KEY env var.
checks.reason: Live OpenAI API key in source code.

checks.description: GitHub personal access token (classic or fine-grained)
checks.fix: Revoke at github.com/settings/tokens. Use GitHub Actions secrets or a vault.
checks.reason: GitHub PAT can be used to push code, read private repos, or impersonate the user.

checks.description: AWS access key ID + secret pattern
checks.fix: Deactivate in AWS IAM console. Use IAM roles or AWS Secrets Manager.
checks.reason: AWS access key committed to repo. Can be used to access cloud resources immediately.

checks.fix: Remove and rotate immediately. Use git-filter-repo to purge from history. Store in a vault.
checks.reason: Private key in source code exposes authentication credentials permanently (even after deletion from git history).

checks.fix: Rotate at dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys. Never commit payment keys.
checks.reason: Stripe secret key allows creating charges and accessing customer data.

checks.description: Google API key or service account credential
checks.fix: Restrict key in Google Cloud Console. Use Application Default Credentials instead.
checks.reason: Google API keys can enable billing charges. Service account JSON grants full GCP access.

checks.description: Slack webhook URL or bot token
checks.fix: Regenerate in api.slack.com/apps. Use Slack secrets management.
checks.reason: Slack tokens allow sending messages as your app or user to all workspaces they belong to.

checks.description: Database connection string with credentials embedded
checks.fix: Use DATABASE_URL env var without the password, or use IAM authentication.
checks.reason: Connection strings with passwords expose database credentials to anyone with repo access.

checks.description: JWT secret or signing key hardcoded
checks.fix: Generate a strong random secret and load from environment: process.env.JWT_SECRET.
checks.reason: Hardcoded JWT secret allows anyone to forge valid tokens for your application.

checks.description: .env file contains non-placeholder values that look like real secrets
checks.fix: Ensure .env is in .gitignore. Commit only .env.example with placeholder values.
checks.reason: .env files with real-looking values may be accidentally committed.

checks.description: Password or secret assigned as string literal in source code
checks.fix: Load all credentials from environment variables. Throw on startup if the variable is missing.
checks.reason: Hardcoded credentials in source code are difficult to rotate and persist in git history.

checks.description: TODO comment referencing credentials or secrets
checks.fix: Resolve the TODO before merging. Do not leave credential management as a future task.
checks.reason: TODOs about secrets often indicate a temporary hardcoded value that was never cleaned up.
